heading: Interest rate reduction on open end consumer credit plans
label: 1665c Interest rate reduction on open end consumer credit plans
notes: Effective Date of 2010 AmendmentAmendment by effective on the designated transfer date, see , set out as a note under , Government Organization and Employees.Pub. L. 111–203section 1100H of Pub. L. 111–203section 552a of Title 5

Effective DateSection effective 9 months after , except as otherwise specifically provided, see , set out as an Effective Date of 2009 Amendment note under .May 22, 2009section 3 of Pub. L. 111–24section 1602 of this title

Effective DateSection effective 9 months after , except as otherwise specifically provided, see , set out as an Effective Date of 2009 Amendment note under .May 22, 2009section 3 of Pub. L. 111–24section 1602 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: If a creditor increases the annual percentage rate applicable to a credit card account under an open end consumer credit plan, based on factors including the credit risk of the obligor, market conditions, or other factors, the creditor shall consider changes in such factors in subsequently determining whether to reduce the annual percentage rate for such obligor.
maintain reasonable methodologies for assessing the factors described in subsection (a);

not less frequently than once every 6 months, review accounts as to which the annual percentage rate has been increased since , to assess whether such factors have changed (including whether any risk has declined);January 1, 2009
reduce the annual percentage rate previously increased when a reduction is indicated by the review; and
in the event of an increase in the annual percentage rate, provide in the written notice required under a statement of the reasons for the increase.section 1637(i) of this title

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: c Rule of construction
children.text: This section shall not be construed to require a reduction in any specific amount.

children.heading: Rulemaking
children.label: d Rulemaking
children.text: The Bureau shall issue final rules not later than 9 months after , to implement the requirements of and evaluate compliance with this section, and subsections (a), (b), and (c) shall become effective 15 months after .1So in original. Probably should be “Board”.1May 22, 2009May 22, 2009
